days.title: April 21 republish — Cottonburr Mortar goes public after canonical-clear regeneration
days.summary: Yesterday's Cottonburr Mortar build was held back because the canonical-clear replay couldn't reproduce in the browser. Today the replay was rewritten as a 14-action fixture that clears the Over the Top board in Chromium, and the April 21 entry was restored to the public archive — so the new plant is finally visible on the site. The planned April 22 feature, Loamspike Burrower, was deliberately slipped per its own spec's hard-prerequisite rule.

days.title: Cottonburr Mortar — Rearmost-Targeting Arc Attack and the Over-the-Top Board
days.summary: Cottonburr Mortar is a new plant that lobs splash shots over the front line and lands on the enemy farthest back. Today's Over the Top challenge pulls Bramble Spear from the roster, so players have to use the new over-the-top throw to reach the threats hiding behind the lead enemy and clear the board before Endless unlocks.

days.title: Amber Wall — First Defender Role Contract and the Hold the Line Board
days.summary: Adds Amber Wall as Rootline Defense's first defender plant, ships the reusable defender-role contract, updates Board Scout to surface the wall's role and stats, and publishes the April 20 Hold the Line board where the previous roster can no longer clear without the new wall.

days.title: Pollen Puff — Second Anti-Air Plant and Reusable Splash Projectile Contract
days.summary: Adds Pollen Puff as the second anti-air plant, ships reusable projectile-level splash (`splash`, `splashRadiusCols`, `splashDamage`), extends Board Scout and observation exports for splash data, and publishes the April 19 Petals in the Wind board where the prior roster loses and Pollen Puff is required to clear.

days.title: Thornwing Moth — First Flying Enemy and `behavior: "flying"` Branch
days.summary: Adds Thornwing Moth as the first flying enemy, introduces a reusable `behavior: "flying"` branch, ships projectile-level anti-air gating via `canHitFlying`, extends Board Scout and observation exports for flying/anti-air data, and publishes the April 18 Wings Over the Garden tutorial/challenge where Thornwings only occupy lanes 1 and 3.

days.title: Cold Lane
days.summary: Rootline Defense's first control plant, Frost Fern, chills a 3-column lane zone so attackers behind it can whittle down slowed enemies — damage and control become two separate levers.

days.title: Briar Sniper: First Ranged Enemy for Rootline Defense
days.summary: Rootline Defense now ships its first ranged enemy. Briar Sniper stops inside the board, aims at a chosen defender for ~0.7s, then fires a thorn bolt that only hits that defender's tile. Plant an attacker between the sniper and its target to screen the shot — support plants don't screen.

days.title: Sunroot Bloom: First Economy Plant for Rootline Defense
days.summary: Rootline Defense now has its first economy plant. Sunroot Bloom costs 50 sap, does not attack, and produces +25 sap pulses. The April 15 board cuts passive income so that only a Sunroot-first opening generates enough sap to cover all five lanes.

days.title: Board Scout: Pre-Run Threat Intel for Rootline Defense
days.summary: The game page now shows you what enemies, plants, and waves are in today's board before you start playing. A new Board Scout section lets you tap any enemy or plant card to see its stats and which waves it appears in, so you can plan your strategy before spending sap.

days.title: Bramble & Thorn — First Two-Plant Roster Day
days.summary: The lane-defense game now offers two plants. Players choose between cheap, steady Thorn Vine and expensive, piercing Bramble Spear — learning when each tool is the right answer across a tutorial and a tighter challenge board.

days.title: The Scoreboard — Visual Judge Score Comparison on Homepage
days.summary: A compact Scoreboard section on the homepage that visualizes how the AI judges scored today's winning candidate across the seven scoring dimensions, making judge disagreement visible at a glance.

days.title: Community Pulse — Emoji Reaction Summary on Homepage
days.summary: A new 'Community Pulse' section on the homepage showing which features got the most love from the community over the last week.

days.title: The homepage now shows the latest pipeline run as a retro terminal
days.summary: Added a terminal-style panel to the homepage that shows what the latest automated update built, tested, and shipped — in a few lines of styled command output.

days.title: The homepage now grows a visual garden of shipped features
days.summary: The homepage now shows a visual garden where each shipped feature appears as a small plant. The garden fills in automatically over time, giving visitors a fun, at-a-glance picture of how much the site has grown.

days.title: Inline Spec Viewer on Day Detail Pages
days.summary: Collapsible technical specification section on day detail pages — read the full spec inline without downloading.

days.title: Garden Vital Stats Homepage Section
days.summary: Dynamic stats bar showing pipeline runs, features shipped, and start date from manifest data.

days.title: "How It Works" pipeline explainer section
days.summary: Add a static, visual step-by-step explainer to the homepage showing the 5-stage autonomous pipeline.
